content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

The amendment identifies a real prompt-context correctness issue: adjacent planned-work context can be fabricated from a stale implicit slot after explicit-ID resolution has already failed, so suppressing or tightening that fallback is the safer and more maintainable behavior.

## Votes

- `fr-3-6b46ccc3`: Accept
  `build_planned_elsewhere_context` currently falls back to `infer_implicit_slot_hint` even when `resolve_bead_plan` has already concluded that membership is unconfirmed. That is safe for true implicit-slot drift cases, but not when the slot has been reassigned to an explicit bead ID: in that case the live bead no longer owns that neighborhood, yet the prompt still injects adjacent summaries from it. Because `plan_hash`/`plan_version` are already withheld in this state, showing those neighbors as nearby planned work is internally inconsistent and can misdirect implementation scope. Gating the fallback to slots that remain implicit/title-fallback-safe, or omitting adjacent neighbors when membership is unconfirmed, is the correct fix. Adding an integration regression around the existing mismatched-explicit-id scenario is also warranted.
